questions.text: Who is the author of 'The Last Lesson'?
questions.explanation: Alphonse Daudet was a French novelist and short-story writer.

questions.text: In which year was the story 'The Last Lesson' set?
questions.explanation: The story is set in the days of the Franco-Prussian War.

questions.text: Which districts of France passed into Prussian hands in the story?
questions.explanation: Following the French defeat, these two border districts were ceded to Prussia.

questions.text: Who is the narrator of 'The Last Lesson'?
questions.explanation: Franz provides a child's perspective on the sudden political and cultural changes.

questions.text: Why was Franz afraid of going to school that morning?
options: He hadn't prepared the lesson on participles
questions.explanation: M. Hamel had said he would question them on participles, and Franz didn't know the first word about them.

questions.text: What was the 'temptation' Franz felt on his way to school?
options: To run away and spend the day outdoors
questions.explanation: The warm weather and the chirping birds were more attractive than rules of grammar.

questions.text: What did Franz see in front of the town hall bulletin-board?
questions.explanation: For two years, all the bad news had come from that bulletin board.

questions.text: Who was the blacksmith who told Franz not to go so fast?
questions.explanation: Wachter was reading the bulletin and knew the news about the schools.

questions.text: What was unusual about the school on that day?
options: It was as quiet as a Sunday morning
questions.explanation: Usually there was a great bustle, but that day everything was still.

questions.text: What was M. Hamel wearing that he only wore on special days?
options: A beautiful green coat and frilled shirt
questions.explanation: He wore his formal Sunday clothes to mark the importance of his final lesson.

questions.text: Who was sitting on the back benches that were usually empty?
options: The village people (Hauser, former mayor, etc.)
questions.explanation: The elders of the village came to show their respect for the teacher and their language.

questions.text: What order had come from Berlin?
options: To teach only German in the schools of Alsace and Lorraine
To recruit students for the army
questions.explanation: This order represents the beginning of cultural and linguistic imposition.

questions.text: M. Hamel told the students that French is the most ________ language in the world.
questions.explanation: He urged them to guard their language among themselves.

questions.text: How did Franz feel about his books after hearing the news?
options: He was glad to be rid of them
They felt like old friends that he couldn't give up
questions.explanation: The realization of loss changed his perspective on his studies and his teacher.

questions.text: What did M. Hamel say when Franz could not recite the rule for the participle?
options: He told him he wouldn't scold him because he must feel bad enough already
He sent him out of the class
questions.explanation: M. Hamel used the moment to reflect on the procrastination of the people of Alsace.

questions.text: Whom did M. Hamel blame for the neglect of learning among the students?
options: The parents, the students, and himself
questions.explanation: He admitted he often sent students to water his flowers or gave them a holiday when he went fishing.

questions.text: M. Hamel said that when a people are enslaved, as long as they hold fast to their language, it is as if:
options: They have the key to their prison
questions.explanation: Language is portrayed as a tool for preserving national identity and survival under occupation.

questions.text: What new copies did M. Hamel bring for the students that day?
options: Copies with 'France, Alsace, France, Alsace' written in a beautiful round hand
questions.explanation: These copies looked like little flags floating everywhere in the schoolroom.

questions.text: What thought came to Franz's mind when he heard the pigeons cooing on the roof?
options: Will they make them sing in German, even the pigeons?
questions.explanation: This reflects the absurdity of trying to impose linguistic control over natural instincts and identity.

questions.text: For how many years had M. Hamel served the school?
questions.explanation: He had stayed in the same place for forty years with his garden outside the window.

questions.text: What sound announced the end of the class?
options: The church clock striking twelve and the Angelus
questions.explanation: The overlapping sounds of the church and the military signified the finality of the moment.

questions.text: What did M. Hamel write on the blackboard at the very end?
questions.explanation: He wrote it as large as he could, using all his strength.

questions.text: What gesture did M. Hamel make to dismiss the class?
options: He leaned his head against the wall and gestured with his hand
questions.explanation: He was too choked with emotion to speak.

questions.text: The phrase 'Viva La France' means:
questions.explanation: It is an expression of French patriotism.

questions.text: The story 'The Last Lesson' highlights which human tendency?
questions.explanation: The story emphasizes how people put off learning until it's too late.

questions.text: What does the 'Last Lesson' symbolize?
questions.explanation: It symbolizes the cultural impact of political defeat.

questions.text: Why were the villagers repenting?
options: For not going to school more often
For not paying the teacher well
questions.explanation: They came to school to thank M. Hamel and expressed regret for their lack of education.

questions.text: What was M. Hamel's sister doing in the room above?
questions.explanation: They had to leave the country the next day.

questions.text: The theme of 'Linguistic Chauvinism' refers to:
options: The unreasonable belief that one's own language is superior
questions.explanation: The story shows the pain of having one's native language taken away by force.

questions.text: Franz was surprised because M. Hamel ________ when he was late.
options: Was very kind and told him to go to his place
Made him stand in the corner
questions.explanation: The teacher's unusual kindness was the first hint that the day was different.

questions.text: What was the blacksmith's real intention when he told Franz there was 'plenty of time'?
options: Because he knew it was the last lesson and school didn't matter anymore
questions.explanation: It was a sarcastic or resigned remark based on the news from Berlin.

questions.text: The story 'The Last Lesson' is written in the ________ person.
questions.explanation: It is narrated by Franz using 'I'.

questions.text: What did M. Hamel compare the French language to?
questions.explanation: He believed language was the ultimate defense for an oppressed nation.

questions.text: Who had been sitting in the back of the room with a three-cornered hat?
questions.explanation: Hauser had even brought an old primer to follow along.

questions.text: Why did M. Hamel stay motionless in his chair during the lesson?
options: He was gazing at everything as if he wanted to fix it in his mind
questions.explanation: He was mentally recording every detail of his home of 40 years.

questions.text: How did the students react to the writing lesson?
options: Everyone set to work and it was very quiet
questions.explanation: Only the scratching of pens over paper could be heard.

questions.text: What does the 'Angelus' refer to?
questions.explanation: The ringing of the Angelus bell coincided with the end of the school day.

questions.text: Why does the story end with a gesture rather than words?
options: Emotion made it impossible for him to speak
questions.explanation: The emotional weight of the 'last lesson' overwhelmed him.

questions.text: What had been the source of all 'bad news' for the people of Alsace?
questions.explanation: News of lost battles and drafts were posted there.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a feeling Franz experienced during the day?
questions.explanation: Franz was deeply saddened and felt a sense of loss for his culture.
